title: Fix vague job description prompt
originalPrompt: Write a job description for a marketing manager. Make it good and attract the right people.
options: Write a job description for a B2B Marketing Manager at a 150-person SaaS company. Include: 3-sentence role summary, 5 key responsibilities (demand gen focus), 5 required qualifications, 2 nice-to-haves, and a salary range (£55k–£70k). Tone: direct, inclusive, no jargon.

Please write a very detailed and compelling job description for a Marketing Manager that will attract great candidates and fully explains everything the role involves and what kind of person we are looking for.
Write a job description for a Marketing Manager.
hint: Specifying company size, specialisation (demand gen), exact section structure, and a salary range means the JD attracts the right candidates and passes a diversity audit.

title: Best interview question generation prompt
originalPrompt: You are hiring a Senior Product Manager. You need behavioural interview questions to assess candidates.
options: Generate 5 behavioural interview questions for a Senior Product Manager role focused on stakeholder management and cross-functional influence. Use the STAR format cue. Include one follow-up probe per question. Avoid questions that could reveal age, family status, or nationality.
Write some interview questions for a Senior Product Manager.

Please create a comprehensive list of interview questions for a Senior Product Manager role that covers all the important competencies and skills we need to assess including product thinking, stakeholder management, data analysis, leadership, and communication.
hint: Fixing the count at 5, specifying the competency focus, requiring follow-up probes, and flagging equality law constraints produces a legally sound, structured interview guide.

title: Rewrite candidate rejection email prompt
originalPrompt: Write a rejection email for the candidates we interviewed but won't be progressing. Keep it nice.
options: Draft a post-interview rejection email for candidates who reached final-stage but were unsuccessful. Acknowledge their time, give one specific positive observation, state the decision clearly without false hope, offer constructive feedback option, and leave the door open for future roles. Warm, professional tone. Max 150 words.
Write a nice rejection email for candidates we interviewed who didn't get the job that will leave them feeling good about our company.
Write a rejection email.
hint: Specifying five structural elements and a 150-word cap produces an email that protects employer brand without being a generic form letter.

title: Concise offer letter key terms prompt
originalPrompt: You need a short prompt to generate the key terms section of a job offer letter.
options: Write the Key Terms section of a job offer letter. Cover: job title, start date, salary (£X), working hours (37.5h/week), notice period (3 months both sides), holiday entitlement (25 days + bank holidays), probation period (6 months), and location (hybrid, 3 days office). Plain English, list format.
Write a job offer letter.

Please write a comprehensive and complete job offer letter that covers all the important terms and conditions of employment including salary, benefits, working arrangements, and all the other key contractual terms the new employee needs to know about.
hint: Listing all eight terms with exact values means no follow-up clarification is needed and the section can be pasted directly into a contract template.

title: Candidate evaluation rubric prompt
originalPrompt: You need to create a structured scoring rubric to evaluate candidates consistently for a Data Analyst role.
options: Create a candidate evaluation rubric for a Data Analyst role. Competencies: SQL proficiency, data visualisation, business communication, analytical thinking, and stakeholder management. For each competency: 4-level rating scale (1=Below Expectations to 4=Exceeds), behavioural anchors per level, and interview evidence expected. Table format.
Build a candidate scoring rubric for our Data Analyst interviews.

Create a comprehensive candidate evaluation framework that covers all the important skills and competencies for the Data Analyst role with clear descriptions of what good and bad performance looks like at interview.
hint: Defining behavioural anchors at each of four levels and specifying the evidence type per competency produces a rubric that ensures inter-rater reliability across a hiring panel.

title: Rewrite headcount approval business case
originalPrompt: I need to write a business case to hire two more people on my team. Help me put something together.
options: Write a headcount approval business case for 2 additional FTE in the Customer Success team. Include: current workload gap (accounts per CSM vs benchmark), revenue at risk (£X churn attributable to coverage gap), cost of hire (fully loaded £X per FTE), payback via NRR improvement, and 3-month ramp plan. Max 1 page.
Write a business case to hire two more people for our Customer Success team explaining why we need them and what value they will add.
Create a detailed business case for adding headcount to our team.
hint: Quantifying the workload gap against a benchmark, linking the hire to churn revenue, and including a ramp plan makes the case finance-committee ready rather than a request without numbers.

title: Quick recruiter screening prompt
originalPrompt: You need a short prompt to generate phone screen questions for an entry-level sales role.
options: Write 5 phone screen questions for an entry-level Sales Development Representative role. Cover: motivation for sales, resilience to rejection, communication style, availability, and salary expectation. One question per topic, no leading questions.
Write phone screen questions for a sales role.

Please create a comprehensive list of phone screening questions for our entry-level SDR role that covers all the important areas we need to assess including motivation, skills, experience, culture fit, and logistics.
hint: Fixing five questions with one named topic per question and banning leading questions produces a structured screen that every recruiter runs consistently.

title: Diversity sourcing strategy prompt
originalPrompt: You want to increase diversity in your engineering hiring pipeline. You need a sourcing strategy.
options: Design a diverse sourcing strategy for engineering roles. Cover: 5 non-traditional sourcing channels (bootcamps, return-to-work programmes, HBCUs, coding non-profits, military transition schemes), adjustments to JD language (remove credential inflation, gender-neutral language audit), structured screening criteria to reduce bias, and a 90-day implementation plan. Practical, specific.
Help me increase diversity in our engineering hiring pipeline.

Write a comprehensive diversity hiring strategy for our engineering team that covers sourcing, job descriptions, interview processes, assessment methods, and all the other changes we need to make to attract and hire more diverse talent.
hint: Naming five specific sourcing channels, requiring JD language adjustments, and a 90-day plan transforms a general diversity goal into a concrete action plan.

title: Fix vague LinkedIn job post prompt
originalPrompt: Write something to post on LinkedIn about our open job. Make it catchy and get people to apply.
options: Write a LinkedIn job post for a Senior UX Designer role at [Company]. Hook: one compelling sentence about the product challenge. Highlight: remote-first culture, equity, and career growth. Call to action: link to apply. Max 150 words. No buzzwords like 'rockstar' or 'ninja'.
Please write a really engaging and exciting LinkedIn post for our open Senior UX Designer position that will attract talented candidates and communicate why our company is a great place to work.
Write a LinkedIn job post.
hint: Specifying the hook, three highlight topics, a word count, and banning specific buzzwords produces a post that resonates with qualified candidates rather than every job seeker.

title: Reference check question prompt
originalPrompt: You are about to make an offer to a candidate but need to complete reference checks first.
options: Generate 6 structured reference check questions for a [Job Title] candidate. Cover: confirmation of role and dates, key strengths relevant to this role, areas for development, management or teamwork style, reason for leaving, and whether the referee would rehire. Open-ended format, no closed yes/no questions.
Write reference check questions for our candidate.

Create a comprehensive reference check question guide that covers all the important areas we should explore with a referee including performance, competencies, team fit, development areas, and anything else that might be relevant.
hint: Fixing six topics in open-ended format and including the re-hire question produces a reference that yields substantive insights rather than dates-of-employment confirmation.

title: Talent acquisition metrics summary
originalPrompt: You need a prompt that produces a monthly talent acquisition dashboard in under 100 tokens.
options: Create a talent acquisition metrics table for last month. Rows: roles open, applications received, phone screens, interviews, offers made, offers accepted, time-to-fill (days), and cost-per-hire. Three columns: This Month, Last Month, Target. No narrative.
Give me a hiring metrics summary.

Please provide a comprehensive monthly talent acquisition dashboard that covers all our key recruiting metrics including applications, pipeline conversion rates, time-to-fill, offer acceptance rates, source of hire, diversity metrics, and cost-per-hire with comparisons to our targets and prior periods.
hint: Fixing eight metric rows with three comparison columns and banning narrative produces a dashboard the CHRO can review in two minutes.

title: Rewrite succession planning framework
originalPrompt: We need a succession planning process for our senior leadership team. Help me build one.
options: Design a succession planning framework for the top 10 leadership roles. For each role: identify 1-2 internal successors (ready now vs 1-2 years), assess development gaps, create a 12-month acceleration plan (assignments, coaching, exposure), and define external pipeline fallback. Output: nine-box readiness grid + individual development table. Annual review cadence.
Help me create a succession planning process for our senior leaders that identifies who could step up into each role if needed.

Build a comprehensive succession planning framework that covers all our key leadership positions and identifies potential successors, assesses their readiness, and creates development plans to prepare them for future roles.
hint: Specifying a nine-box readiness grid, a 12-month acceleration plan per successor, and an external fallback produces a succession plan that drives real development activity rather than just a list of names.

title: Hiring manager briefing prompt
originalPrompt: Before starting a search for a new hire, you need to brief the recruiting team on the role.
options: Write a hiring manager briefing document for a [Job Title] role. Include: business reason for the hire, team context, must-have vs nice-to-have skills, compensation range, interview process stages, and top 3 qualities that will make someone successful in this role. 1 page max.
Write a briefing for the recruiter about the role we want to fill.

Create a comprehensive hiring manager briefing that covers everything the recruitment team needs to know about the role including the full job requirements, competencies, culture fit factors, compensation, interview process, timeline, and business context.
hint: Listing six specific elements including must-have vs nice-to-have and a success profile gives recruiters a precise brief that improves sourcing accuracy from day one.

title: Fix vague training module prompt
originalPrompt: Create a training module for our new managers. Cover the important management skills they need.
options: Create a training module outline for first-time managers. Sections: (1) Transitioning from individual contributor to manager, (2) Giving and receiving feedback, (3) Running effective 1:1s, (4) Setting goals and managing performance, (5) Managing conflict. Each section: learning objective, 3 key content points, one activity, and estimated duration. Total module: 3 hours.
Please create a comprehensive and detailed training module for our new managers that covers all the essential management skills they need to be successful in their roles.
Write a new manager training programme.
hint: Defining five specific sections with four elements per section and a total time budget means the module is ready for an instructional designer to build, not plan.

title: Learning objective generation prompt
originalPrompt: You are designing a data literacy workshop for non-technical employees.
options: Write 4 learning objectives for a 2-hour data literacy workshop for non-technical employees. Use Bloom's taxonomy action verbs (understand, apply, analyse, evaluate). Each objective: 'By the end of this session, participants will be able to [verb] [specific skill or knowledge].' Focus on reading charts, spotting trends, and questioning data sources.
Write learning objectives for our data literacy workshop.

Create comprehensive learning objectives for our data literacy training programme that covers all the data skills our non-technical employees need to develop to be effective in a data-driven organisation.
hint: Using Bloom's taxonomy verbs, the standard learning objective stem, and naming the three skill focus areas produces objectives that can be directly assessed at the end of the session.

title: Rewrite onboarding checklist prompt
originalPrompt: Create an onboarding checklist for new hires. Make sure it's thorough and covers everything they need.
options: Create a 90-day onboarding checklist for a new hire. Phases: Week 1 (admin, access, meet the team), Month 1 (role foundations, shadow key processes, first deliverable), Day 30–90 (own workstream, build relationships, 30/60/90 check-in with manager). Each phase: 5–7 concrete checkbox actions with responsible party (new hire or manager).
Please write a very comprehensive and thorough onboarding checklist that covers absolutely everything a new employee needs to do and know in their first few months to get up to speed quickly.
Write a new hire onboarding checklist.
hint: A three-phase structure with 5–7 actions per phase and a responsible party column makes the checklist usable by both the new hire and the manager without ambiguity.

title: Concise workshop facilitation guide
originalPrompt: You need a tight prompt to generate a workshop facilitation guide section in under 75 tokens.
options: Write a facilitator guide section for a 45-min workshop activity on 'giving effective feedback'. Include: setup instructions (time, materials, group size), activity steps (numbered), facilitator prompts (2 discussion questions), and debrief (key takeaway to land). Concise, facilitator-facing language.
Write a facilitation guide for our feedback workshop activity.

Please write a comprehensive and detailed facilitator guide for this workshop activity that gives the facilitator everything they need to run the session effectively including all the setup requirements, step-by-step instructions, discussion questions, and debrief guidance.
hint: Fixing four structural elements (setup, steps, prompts, debrief) and specifying facilitator-facing language produces a guide that works as a run-of-show reference.

title: Skills gap analysis prompt
originalPrompt: Your organisation wants to build a future skills capability map to identify training priorities.
options: Create a skills gap analysis framework for a 200-person professional services firm. Steps: (1) define future-state skills required per role family (list 3 role families), (2) current state self-assessment scale (1–4 per skill), (3) gap identification (target vs current), (4) prioritisation matrix (impact on business vs ease of build), (5) learning pathway recommendations per gap. Output as a structured framework with example data.
Build a skills gap analysis for our organisation.
Create a comprehensive skills gap analysis that identifies all the skill gaps across our organisation and recommends training and development actions to close them.
hint: A five-step framework with specific outputs at each step (prioritisation matrix, learning pathways) and example data produces a tool the L&D team can populate, not design from scratch.

title: Rewrite e-learning course outline prompt
originalPrompt: Write an e-learning course on unconscious bias. It should be good for all staff.
options: Write an e-learning course outline on unconscious bias for a diverse corporate workforce. Modules: (1) What is unconscious bias (with 3 real-world examples), (2) Types (affinity, attribution, confirmation), (3) Impact on hiring and performance reviews, (4) Practical mitigation strategies (structured decisions, diverse panels). Each module: learning objective, content outline, one interactive element, estimated time (total course ≤45 min).
Create a comprehensive e-learning course on unconscious bias that covers all aspects of the topic and engages all staff regardless of their background or experience.
Write an unconscious bias e-learning course for all employees.
hint: Defining four modules with specific bias types, a work-context application (hiring, reviews), and a 45-minute cap means the course is focused and commercially deliverable.

title: Quick pre-reading prompt for a training
originalPrompt: You need a short prompt to generate a pre-reading brief for an upcoming leadership training day.
options: Write a pre-reading brief for a leadership training day on psychological safety. Cover: definition (1 sentence), why it matters (1 statistic or example), 3 reflection questions for participants to consider before attending. Max 200 words.
Write pre-reading for our leadership training.

Please create a comprehensive pre-reading pack for participants attending our leadership training day on psychological safety that covers the background theory, key research, practical examples, and reflection exercises to help them get the most out of the session.
hint: A definition, one evidence anchor, and three reflection questions in 200 words is enough to prime participants without replacing the session content.

title: L&D ROI measurement prompt
originalPrompt: Your L&D team is under pressure to demonstrate the return on investment of training programmes.
options: Design an ROI measurement framework for a sales training programme. Apply Kirkpatrick Levels 1–4: Level 1 (participant satisfaction survey, 5 questions), Level 2 (pre/post knowledge test, 10 MCQ), Level 3 (manager observation form at 30/90 days, 5 behaviours), Level 4 (sales metrics: quota attainment, ramp time before/after). Include data collection timing and owner per level.
Help me measure the ROI of our training programmes.
Create a comprehensive learning evaluation framework that covers all four levels of the Kirkpatrick model and shows how we can measure the impact of our training investments on business performance.
hint: Naming specific measurement instruments at each Kirkpatrick level with data collection timing and owners means the framework is implementable from day one, not just theoretically sound.

title: Fix vague coaching conversation prompt
originalPrompt: Help me have a coaching conversation with an employee about their performance. Give me some questions to ask.
options: Generate a coaching conversation guide for a manager discussing performance concerns with a direct report. Use the GROW model: 5 Goal-setting questions, 3 Reality questions (to understand current state), 3 Options questions (to explore solutions), 2 Will questions (to confirm commitment). Avoid leading or judgmental language.
Write some coaching questions I can use when talking to my employee about their performance issues.

Create a comprehensive set of coaching questions for a performance conversation that covers all the important areas including understanding the employee's perspective, exploring options for improvement, and agreeing on an action plan.
hint: Structuring questions around the GROW model with specific counts per stage and a language directive produces a coaching guide that follows evidence-based methodology.

title: Manager development programme prompt
originalPrompt: You are designing a 6-month development programme for high-potential mid-level managers.
options: Design a 6-month manager development programme for 15 high-potential managers. Structure: monthly learning themes (strategic thinking, commercial acumen, leading change, coaching skills, exec presence, innovation), format per month (workshop, peer group, senior leader session, self-directed), expected commitment (4h/month), and cohort size justification. Output: programme calendar table.
Create a development programme for our high-potential managers.

Design a comprehensive management development programme for our high-potential leaders that covers all the important leadership and management skills they need to develop to be ready for senior roles.
hint: Naming six specific monthly themes, specifying format variety per month, and requesting a programme calendar table produces a proposal the L&D team can cost and schedule.

title: Fix vague policy update comms prompt
originalPrompt: Write a message to tell staff about the changes to our holiday policy. Keep it short and clear.
options: Write a staff announcement about a change to the annual leave policy. Cover: what is changing (entitlement increasing from 25 to 28 days), effective date, how to apply the change (update in [HR system]), any carryover rule change, and who to contact for questions. Plain English, max 200 words, positive tone.
Please write a clear and comprehensive communication to all staff explaining the changes we are making to our holiday policy and why we are making them and how it will affect them.
Announce the holiday policy change to staff.
hint: Including the specific change (25 to 28 days), effective date, action required, and contact info means staff have everything they need in one message without needing to follow up.

title: HR process documentation prompt
originalPrompt: You need to document your grievance procedure so employees and managers can follow it correctly.
options: Document the employee grievance procedure as a step-by-step process guide. Cover: informal resolution (who, what, when), formal grievance submission (form required, 5-day acknowledgement), investigation steps (impartial manager, right to be accompanied), outcome and appeal rights, and timeline (max 28 days end-to-end). Include a flowchart in text. Both employee and manager versions.
Write a grievance procedure document for employees and managers.

Create a comprehensive grievance procedure document that covers every step of the formal and informal grievance process in detail so both employees and managers know exactly what to do when a grievance is raised.
hint: Covering informal and formal routes, specifying the legal right to accompaniment, fixing a 28-day timeline, and requesting both employee and manager versions means the document is legally compliant and usable by both parties.

title: Rewrite offboarding checklist prompt
originalPrompt: Create an offboarding checklist for employees who are leaving. Make sure we cover everything.
options: Create an offboarding checklist for a departing employee. Phases: Notice period (knowledge transfer plan, handover document), Final week (system access revocation, equipment return, payroll finalisation), Post-departure (reference request process, alumni network invite, exit interview). Responsible party per item (HR, IT, Line Manager, Employee). Checkbox format.
Please create a comprehensive offboarding checklist that covers all the tasks and activities that need to happen when an employee leaves our company.
Write an employee offboarding checklist.
hint: Three time-phased sections with responsible parties per item means the checklist drives action across IT, HR, and management without tasks falling through the gaps.

title: Quick absence management policy
originalPrompt: You need a short prompt to draft the Scope section of an absence management policy.
options: Write the Scope section of an absence management policy. Cover: who it applies to (all UK employees, excluding contractors), types of absence covered (sick leave, unauthorised, persistent short-term), what triggers a formal process (Bradford Factor score >200), and who is responsible (line manager and HR). Max 150 words.
Write a scope section for our absence policy.

Please write a comprehensive scope section for our absence management policy that clearly defines all the different types of employees it covers, all the categories of absence it applies to, the thresholds that trigger formal action, and all the responsibilities of managers, HR, and employees.
hint: Naming a specific trigger threshold (Bradford Factor >200) and defining four content elements in 150 words produces a scope section that is precise and legally auditable.

title: HRIS configuration requirements prompt
originalPrompt: You are about to implement a new HRIS and need to specify your requirements for the vendor demo.
options: Write an HRIS requirements specification for a 300-person company. Sections: core HR (employee records, org chart, document management), payroll integration (bi-directional sync with [payroll system]), self-service (employee leave requests, manager approvals), reporting (headcount, attrition, pay equity), and compliance (UK right-to-work checks, GDPR data residency). For each section: must-have requirements and nice-to-haves.
Write requirements for our new HR system.
Create a comprehensive HRIS requirements document that covers all the functional and technical requirements we need our new HR information system to meet across all areas of HR and people operations.
hint: Five named modules with must-have vs nice-to-have classification and specific compliance requirements gives the vendor enough detail to run a meaningful demo rather than a generic walkthrough.

title: Rewrite redundancy process prompt
originalPrompt: We might need to make some redundancies. Help me think through the process we need to follow.
options: Outline a UK statutory redundancy consultation process for a proposed reduction of 12 roles across two departments. Cover: at-risk notification requirements, collective consultation threshold (20+ roles = 45-day minimum), selection criteria (skills-based, objective), alternative roles offer obligation, statutory redundancy pay calculation formula, and appeal process. Legal compliance first, practical format.
Help me understand the redundancy process we need to follow when making employees redundant.
Create a comprehensive guide to the redundancy process covering all the legal requirements, consultation obligations, selection criteria, notification requirements, and financial entitlements.
hint: Specifying 12 roles, the collective consultation trigger at 20+, and requiring the statutory pay formula means the outline is legally accurate for the specific scenario rather than generically correct.

title: Quick parental leave policy summary
originalPrompt: You need a concise prompt to summarise your enhanced parental leave policy for a new joiner.
options: Summarise our enhanced parental leave policy for a new employee in 5 bullets. Cover: maternity (weeks at full pay vs statutory), paternity (weeks and pay), shared parental leave eligibility, adoption leave, and how to notify HR. Plain English, no legalese.
Summarise our parental leave policy.

Please write a comprehensive summary of our enhanced parental leave policy that covers all aspects of maternity, paternity, shared parental leave, adoption leave, and any other family leave entitlements for new employees joining our company.
hint: Five bullets with named leave types, specific pay comparisons, and a process step means the summary is self-contained for a new joiner without referencing the full policy document.

title: People analytics dashboard prompt
originalPrompt: The CHRO wants a monthly people analytics dashboard to spot workforce trends early.
options: Design a monthly people analytics dashboard for a CHRO. Metrics: headcount (total, by department, by tenure band), voluntary attrition (rolling 12-month, by department, regrettable vs non-regrettable), time-to-fill (by role level), engagement score (eNPS trend), and pay equity ratio (gender and ethnicity). For each metric: definition, data source, RAG threshold, and visualisation type. Executive-facing format.
Build a people analytics dashboard for our CHRO.

Create a comprehensive people analytics dashboard that covers all the important workforce metrics the CHRO needs to monitor including headcount, attrition, hiring, engagement, performance, and pay equity.
hint: Defining five specific metrics with four sub-elements each (definition, source, RAG threshold, visualisation) means the dashboard specification is ready for a data team to build without further scoping.

title: Fix vague flexible working policy prompt
originalPrompt: Write a flexible working policy. It should explain what options we offer and how to request them.
options: Write a flexible working policy for a hybrid-first company. Cover: eligible arrangements (compressed hours, part-time, remote-first, job share), eligibility criteria (minimum 26 weeks' service), request process (written application, 3-month trial), grounds for refusal (UK Flexible Working Act basis), and manager responsibilities. Clear, plain English, numbered clauses.
Please write a comprehensive flexible working policy that clearly explains all the flexible working options available and how employees can apply for them.
Write a flexible working policy.
hint: Naming four arrangement types, the 26-week eligibility threshold, and citing the legal grounds for refusal produces a policy that is both employee-friendly and legally sound.

title: Exit interview questions prompt
originalPrompt: You want to gather meaningful insights from departing employees. You need structured exit interview questions.
options: Generate 8 exit interview questions to diagnose turnover drivers. Cover: primary reason for leaving, what would have made them stay, rating of manager relationship, experience of company culture, adequacy of growth and development opportunities, recommendation likelihood (NPS-style), and feedback on the onboarding experience. Open-ended, non-leading, 1 question per topic.
Write exit interview questions for employees leaving our company.
Create a comprehensive exit interview questionnaire that covers all the important areas we want to understand about why employees are leaving and what we could do to improve retention.
hint: Fixing eight questions mapped to seven specific turnover drivers and including an NPS-style question produces data that feeds directly into a retention analysis.

title: Fix vague engagement survey prompt
originalPrompt: Write some questions for our employee engagement survey. We want to know how people feel.
options: Write 10 employee engagement survey questions across 5 dimensions: role clarity (2 questions), manager effectiveness (2 questions), team collaboration (2 questions), growth and development (2 questions), and wellbeing (2 questions). Use a 1–5 Likert scale. One of each pair should be positively and one negatively framed to reduce acquiescence bias.

Please write comprehensive and varied employee engagement survey questions that cover all the important aspects of the employee experience and will give us useful insights into how our people are feeling.
Write employee engagement survey questions.
hint: Defining five dimensions with two questions each, specifying the Likert scale, and requiring paired positive/negative framing produces a survey with built-in bias control.

title: Team retrospective prompt generation
originalPrompt: Your team has just completed a major project and you want to run a retrospective to learn from the experience.
options: Design a 60-minute team retrospective session for a post-project review with 8 people. Activities: (1) 'Sailboat' exercise (10 min): wind = what helped, anchor = what slowed us, rocks = risks ahead; (2) Dot voting on top 3 improvements (10 min); (3) Action planning (20 min): 3 actions with owner and deadline; (4) Appreciation round (5 min). Facilitator instructions per activity.
Create a retrospective agenda for our team to review the completed project.

Write a comprehensive team retrospective plan that covers all the different aspects of the project we want to reflect on and includes activities that will help the team identify what went well, what to improve, and what actions to take going forward.
hint: Naming the specific retrospective technique (Sailboat), fixing timing per activity, and including facilitator instructions means the session can be run without additional preparation.

title: Rewrite values communication prompt
originalPrompt: Write something to communicate our company values to the team. We want them to live the values.
options: Write an internal communication to bring our 4 company values to life for employees. For each value [list values]: one concrete example of the behaviour in action at our company, one behaviour that would contradict it, and one question employees can ask themselves daily to live it. Tone: authentic, not corporate. Max 400 words.
Write an inspiring communication about our company values that will motivate employees to embrace and demonstrate the values in their daily work.
Create a communication about our company values.
hint: Asking for a positive example, a counter-example, and a daily self-check question per value produces a communication that translates abstract values into observable behaviours.

title: Quick pulse survey prompt
originalPrompt: You need a short prompt to generate a 5-question monthly pulse survey for employees.
options: Write a 5-question monthly pulse survey for employees. Topics: overall engagement (NPS-style), workload manageability, clarity on priorities, confidence in leadership, and one open-ended question. Likert scale (1–5) for closed questions. Max 1 sentence per question.
Write a pulse survey.

Please create a comprehensive monthly pulse survey that covers all the important aspects of employee experience and engagement including satisfaction, wellbeing, manager relationships, workload, growth, and company direction.
hint: Fixing five topics with specified question types (Likert vs open-ended) and a one-sentence cap per question produces a pulse survey that takes under 2 minutes to complete.

title: Recognition programme design prompt
originalPrompt: You want to launch a peer recognition programme to improve team morale and reinforce company values.
options: Design a peer recognition programme for a 200-person company. Cover: recognition mechanics (peer nominations via [tool], monthly cadence), reward tiers (spot recognition = £25 gift card, monthly winner = £150 experience, annual winner = £500 + exec lunch), values linkage (nomination must cite one of 4 company values + specific behaviour), manager visibility, and success metrics (participation rate target 70%). Implementation plan: 90 days.
Design a peer recognition programme for our company.

Create a comprehensive employee recognition programme that includes both peer and manager recognition, monetary and non-monetary rewards, links to company values, and clear processes for nominations, selection, and communication.
hint: Specifying three reward tiers with exact values, a values-linkage requirement, a 70% participation target, and a 90-day plan produces a programme the People team can launch, not concept from scratch.

title: Rewrite employer brand campaign prompt
originalPrompt: We need to improve our employer brand. Help me think through a campaign to attract talent.
options: Design a 3-month employer brand campaign for a 300-person tech company targeting senior software engineers. Channels: LinkedIn (4 posts/week), Glassdoor (review response strategy), engineering blog (2 posts/month), and referral programme. Content themes: engineering culture, tech stack, flexibility. Success metrics: Glassdoor score improvement, LinkedIn follower growth, referral hire rate. Output: campaign calendar and messaging framework.
Help me create an employer brand campaign to attract better talent to our company.
Create a comprehensive employer branding strategy that covers all the channels we need to use and all the content we need to create to improve how we are perceived as an employer in the market.
hint: Specifying the target persona (senior engineers), four named channels with frequency, three content themes, and three success metrics means the campaign has a measurable brief from day one.

title: DEI initiative proposal prompt
originalPrompt: You need a tight prompt to propose a new DEI initiative to your leadership team in under 100 tokens.
options: Write a one-page DEI initiative proposal for leadership. Include: problem statement (1 sentence with data), proposed initiative name and description, target population, success metric (with baseline and 12-month target), resource required (£ and FTE), and risk if we do not act. Factual, no jargon.
Propose a DEI initiative for our company.

Please write a comprehensive and detailed proposal for a new diversity, equity, and inclusion initiative that covers the business case, the specific initiative design, the target groups it will benefit, the resource requirements, the success metrics, the implementation plan, and the risks of inaction.
hint: A six-element structure in one-page format with baseline and target metrics gives leadership the information needed for a go/no-go decision without a lengthy appendix.

title: Culture diagnostic framework prompt
originalPrompt: Post-merger, you need to assess the cultural alignment between two organisations before designing an integration programme.
options: Design a post-merger culture diagnostic framework for two organisations. Dimensions: values alignment, leadership style (command-control vs empowering), decision-making norms, collaboration and communication patterns, and attitudes to risk and innovation. Methods per dimension: leader interviews (5 questions), all-staff survey (3 Likert items), and artefact review (policies, rituals). Output: RAG scoring matrix and integration risk summary.
Help me assess the cultural alignment between our two companies after the merger.

Create a comprehensive culture assessment framework that compares all aspects of the two organisations' cultures and identifies the key areas of alignment and conflict that will need to be addressed during the integration.

title: Fix vague team event ideas prompt

Give me team event ideas.

title: Pay equity audit prompt

options: Draft an international assignment policy for a UK employee relocating to Singapore for 12 months. Include: levelling criteria table and a one-page guide for managers doing calibrations. Compatible with market salary data.
Build a job levelling framework for our company.
Create a comprehensive job levelling system that clearly defines the criteria for each level, helps managers grade roles consistently, and supports our career development and compensation programmes.
hint: Eight levels with four defined criteria each, example titles, and a manager calibration guide produces a framework that drives both compensation decisions and career conversations.

title: Rewrite pension communication prompt
originalPrompt: Write something to explain our pension scheme to employees. A lot of them don't engage with it.
options: Write a plain-English pension guide for employees under 35 who are not actively engaged with their pension. Cover: how auto-enrolment works, employer match (5% employer, 3% employee), what the money is invested in (default fund), how to increase contributions, and how a £100/month increase now could affect retirement pot (use a 25-year compound growth example). Engaging, future-focused tone.
Write a guide to our pension scheme for employees that explains how it works and encourages them to engage with it.

Create a comprehensive pension education resource that explains all aspects of the company pension scheme including contribution rates, investment options, tax advantages, retirement projections, and how to make changes to contributions.
hint: Targeting under-35s specifically, using a concrete compound growth example, and specifying the exact match rates makes the pension communication relevant and motivating for the least-engaged cohort.

title: Concise market pricing summary
originalPrompt: You need a tight prompt to produce a market pay analysis summary for one role.
options: Summarise market pay data for [Job Title] in [City]. Show: P25, P50, P75, and P90 base salary from [data source], our current midpoint (£X), and whether we are above/below/at market. One recommendation sentence. Table format. No narrative.
Give me market pay data for this role.

Please provide a comprehensive market pay analysis for this role including all available salary data points, comparison to our internal pay ranges, analysis of the talent market, and recommendations for any pay adjustments needed to remain competitive.
hint: Four percentile points, a comparison to internal midpoint, and a one-sentence recommendation in table format gives the HR business partner everything needed for a pay review discussion in minimal tokens.

title: Flexible benefits platform brief prompt
originalPrompt: You are briefing a benefits platform vendor on your requirements for a flexible benefits scheme.
options: Write a flexible benefits platform requirements brief for a 300-person company. Required features: benefit allowance management (£X per employee per year), choice of 10+ benefit categories (health, wellbeing, financial, lifestyle), P11D reporting, SSO integration with existing HR system, employee mobile app, and real-time utilisation dashboard for HR. Must-have vs nice-to-have classification. Procurement tone.
Write a brief for a flexible benefits platform vendor.
Create a comprehensive requirements document for our flexible benefits platform that covers all the functionality we need and all the technical and integration requirements the vendor needs to meet.
hint: Six named features with must-have/nice-to-have classification and specific technical requirements (P11D, SSO) means vendors can confirm fit before a demo rather than wasting everyone's time.

title: Probation review meeting guide prompt
originalPrompt: You need to prepare line managers to run structured probation review meetings.
options: Write a manager guide for conducting a 3-month probation review meeting. Structure: review objectives set at start, assess performance against each (Met/Partially Met/Not Met), discuss one strength and one development area with specific behavioural examples, confirm probation outcome (pass/extend/fail) with clear rationale, and document agreed actions. Include: what to say for each outcome, legal considerations (right to appeal), and HR escalation triggers. 1 page.
Write a guide for managers running probation reviews.

Create a comprehensive probation review guide for managers that covers how to prepare for and conduct the review meeting, how to assess performance fairly, how to communicate the outcome, and how to support employees who need additional time.
hint: Scripting what to say for each outcome and flagging the appeal right means managers are legally prepared as well as conversationally equipped.

title: Rewrite HR project update email
originalPrompt: Write an update email about our HR transformation project for the team. Keep them in the loop.
options: Write a project update email to the HR team about the HRIS implementation. Cover: progress this month (3 bullet milestones achieved), what is happening next month (2 upcoming milestones), one risk and mitigation, any decisions or input needed from the team, and a thank you to contributors. Max 200 words, engaging tone.
Write an update email about our HR project for the team.

Please write a comprehensive and detailed update email about our HR transformation project that covers all the progress we have made, all the upcoming activities, all the risks and issues, and what the team needs to do next.
hint: Three achieved milestones, two upcoming milestones, one risk, and a clear call for input in 200 words keeps the team informed without burying them in status updates.

title: Quick HR metric definition
originalPrompt: You need a short prompt to get clear definitions for three HR metrics.
options: Define these 3 HR metrics: voluntary attrition rate, time-to-fill, and eNPS. For each: definition, formula (if applicable), and why it matters. One paragraph per metric. Plain English.
Define our HR metrics.

Please provide comprehensive and detailed explanations of all our key HR metrics including how they are calculated, what they tell us about the health of our workforce, how they compare to industry benchmarks, and what actions we can take to improve them.
hint: Naming the three metrics and requiring definition, formula, and business relevance per metric produces a usable HR glossary entry in minimal output.

title: Rewrite internal mobility policy prompt
originalPrompt: Write a policy on internal mobility. We want employees to be able to move around the company.
options: Draft an internal mobility policy for a 400-person company. Cover: eligibility to apply for internal roles (minimum 12 months in current role, manager notification required), application process (via internal job board, priority over external candidates), transfer package (no change to terms unless promotion, no re-probation), and what happens if the application is unsuccessful (feedback within 5 days, one appeal right). Encouraging, transparent tone.
Write an internal mobility policy that makes it easy for employees to move between teams and roles.
Create a comprehensive internal mobility policy that covers all the rules and processes for employees who want to apply for other roles within the company.
hint: A 12-month tenure requirement, a priority-over-external policy, and a defined feedback timeline gives employees clarity on the process and encourages them to use it.

title: Rewrite EVP development prompt
originalPrompt: Help me define our employer value proposition. We need to be able to tell candidates why they should join us.
options: Develop an Employer Value Proposition (EVP) for a 300-person B2B SaaS company. Framework: (1) research inputs (exit interview themes, engagement survey top drivers, candidate feedback), (2) 5 EVP pillars (mission impact, career growth, flexibility, team quality, reward), (3) differentiated proof points per pillar (specific, not generic), (4) one-sentence EVP statement, (5) activation plan (careers site, JDs, interview conversations). Output: EVP framework and statement.
Help me write our employer value proposition that explains why people should work here.
Create a comprehensive employer value proposition that clearly articulates what makes our company a great place to work and differentiates us from our competitors in the talent market.
hint: A five-stage framework ending in a one-sentence EVP statement and an activation plan means the EVP is both strategically grounded and operationally deployable.

title: Coaching culture programme prompt
originalPrompt: Your organisation wants to shift from a directive management style to a coaching culture.
options: Design a 12-month coaching culture programme for 50 people managers. Phased approach: Phase 1 (Months 1–3): coaching skills workshop (GROW model, active listening, 16 hours), Phase 2 (Months 4–9): peer coaching triads (bi-monthly, observed practice), Phase 3 (Months 10–12): measure and embed (360 feedback on coaching behaviours, integration into PDR process). Senior leader sponsorship required. Success metric: 30% improvement in 'growth and development' pulse question.
Design a coaching culture programme for our managers.
Create a comprehensive programme to develop coaching skills across our management population that will help them shift from telling to asking and create a more empowering and developmental culture.
hint: A three-phase structure with hours, methods, and a specific success metric (30% pulse improvement) means the programme has clear investment, activity, and outcome expectations.

title: Rewrite new manager transition prompt
originalPrompt: Write content for new managers who have just been promoted from individual contributor. Help them with the transition.
options: Write a first-30-days guide for a newly promoted manager transitioning from individual contributor. Week 1: listen and observe (do not change anything), Week 2: 1:1s with each team member (questions: workload, blockers, what to keep/change), Week 3: share initial priorities with team, Week 4: first team meeting as facilitator (not presenter). Common pitfalls sidebar: still doing IC work, over-supervising, not asking for help. Practical, direct tone.
Write advice for our new managers who have been promoted from individual contributor roles.
Create a comprehensive guide for employees who have been promoted to management that covers all the mindset shifts and practical changes they need to make to be successful in their new role.
hint: A week-by-week structure with a specific focus per week and a common pitfalls sidebar produces a guide that prevents the most damaging new-manager mistakes rather than describing what good looks like.

title: Rewrite HR strategy document prompt
originalPrompt: Write our HR strategy for next year. We need something to present to the leadership team.
options: Draft a 3-year HR strategy document for a 400-person company targeting 2x growth. Structure: context (talent market headwinds, growth implications for headcount), strategic pillars (attract: EVP and sourcing; develop: skills and leadership pipeline; retain: culture and reward; operate: HRIS and HR service model), OKRs per pillar (2 objectives, 3 KRs each), investment summary (headcount and budget), and quick wins in Year 1. Exec presentation format, 10 slides.
Write our HR strategy for leadership.
Create a comprehensive HR strategy that covers all aspects of people management and development for the next 3 years and demonstrates how the HR function will support the company's growth ambitions.
hint: Four named strategic pillars with OKRs, an investment summary, and a 10-slide format constraint means the strategy is boardroom-ready and finance-approved before presentation.

title: Fix vague benefits FAQ prompt
originalPrompt: Write a FAQ about our employee benefits so people know what they are entitled to.
options: Write a benefits FAQ for employees covering the 8 most common questions. Include: how to enrol, who is eligible for health insurance (employee only vs family), pension opt-out process, when salary review happens, how to claim expenses, gym benefit activation, parental leave request process, and who to contact for benefits queries. Q&A format, plain English.
Please write a comprehensive and helpful FAQ document about all our employee benefits that answers all the questions employees might have about their entitlements.
Write a benefits FAQ.
hint: Fixing eight specific questions based on real employee queries means the FAQ reduces HR inbox volume by addressing the actual pain points rather than what HR thinks employees ask.

title: Change management comms prompt
originalPrompt: You need to prepare employees for a significant change to the performance management process.
options: Write an all-staff communication introducing a change from annual performance reviews to continuous feedback and quarterly check-ins. Cover: why the process is changing (research on annual reviews), what the new process looks like (quarterly manager 1:1 with structured agenda, peer feedback option), what employees need to do now (book Q1 1:1 with manager), and where to get support (L&D resource hub). Tone: confident, change-positive. Max 300 words.
Communicate the change to our performance management process to all employees.

Write a comprehensive communication about our new performance management approach that explains all the changes, the reasons behind them, how the new process works, what training is available, and what employees and managers need to do.
hint: Giving the evidence basis for the change, the specific new mechanics, and one clear action ('book your Q1 1:1') with a support signpost produces a communication that builds buy-in rather than just announcing a change.

title: Concise sourcing channel comparison
originalPrompt: You need a tight prompt to compare recruiting channels for a specific role.
options: Compare 4 sourcing channels for a Senior Data Engineer role: LinkedIn Recruiter, specialist job board (e.g. Otta), employee referral programme, and direct approach via GitHub. For each: typical cost-per-hire, quality of candidates, time-to-first-response, and fit for this role (H/M/L). Table format. No narrative.
Compare our recruiting channels for this role.

Please provide a comprehensive analysis of all the different sourcing channels available for recruiting a Senior Data Engineer, including their costs, effectiveness, quality of candidates produced, time requirements, and fit with our employer brand.
hint: Four channels with four defined columns and a table-only constraint produces a sourcing decision matrix in a fraction of the tokens a narrative analysis would require.

title: HR operating model design prompt
originalPrompt: You are redesigning the HR operating model to support a company scaling from 200 to 600 employees over 3 years.
options: Design an HR operating model for scaling from 200 to 600 employees over 3 years. Components: HRBP model (ratio, responsibilities at each headcount milestone), HR shared services (centralised admin, tier-0 self-service, tier-1 HR ops), CoE structure (Talent Acquisition, L&D, Reward, People Analytics), HRIS infrastructure requirements, and HR headcount plan (current 4 FTE to future X FTE by year). Output: operating model diagram in text + implementation roadmap.
Help me design our HR operating model for our scale-up.

Create a comprehensive HR operating model that covers all aspects of how the HR function should be structured and resourced to support our organisation as we grow significantly over the next few years.
hint: Defining three model components (HRBP, shared services, CoE), headcount milestones, and requesting a roadmap produces an operating model that the board can fund and the HR team can build against.

title: Induction schedule design prompt
originalPrompt: You need to design the first day schedule for a new employee joining a remote-first company.
options: Design a first-day schedule for a remote new joiner. Agenda: 9am IT setup call with IT (45 min), 10am 1:1 with manager (30 min, welcome and role overview), 11am virtual team intro (30 min), 12pm lunch break, 1pm company overview session (L&D recorded video, 1h), 2:30pm buddy introduction call (30 min), 3pm self-directed reading (handbook, tools, org chart). Include: prework manager must complete before day 1.
Write a first day plan for a new remote employee.

Create a comprehensive first day onboarding schedule for our new remote employee that ensures they feel welcomed and informed and covers all the introductions, systems setup, company orientation, and other activities they need to complete.
hint: A timed agenda with specific session formats and a pre-work callout for the manager means the new joiner's first day runs smoothly without last-minute scrambling.

title: Fix vague return to work prompt
originalPrompt: Write a return to work plan for an employee coming back from long-term sick leave. Make sure it's supportive.
options: Write a phased return-to-work plan for an employee returning after 3 months' long-term sickness absence. Cover: phased hours schedule (Week 1: 50%, Week 2: 75%, Week 3: full time), adjusted duties during phased return, weekly check-in with manager, trigger for referral to occupational health, and employee's right to request adjustments under the Equality Act. Supportive, non-judgmental tone.

Please write a comprehensive and supportive return to work plan for our employee who has been on long-term sick leave that covers how we will support them back into the workplace gradually and considerately.
Write a return to work plan.
hint: A three-week phased hours schedule with specific percentages and an Equality Act reference means the plan is both practically workable and legally compliant.

title: Concise values storybook entry
originalPrompt: You need a tight prompt to create a values storybook entry based on a real employee example.
options: Write a values storybook entry for our internal comms. Format: employee name and role (placeholder), situation they faced (1 sentence), action they took that demonstrates [Value Name], and outcome for the business or customer (1 sentence). Total: 4 sentences, engaging and authentic tone.
Write a values story.

Please write a comprehensive and inspiring values story about an employee who demonstrated one of our company values in an exceptional way, including the background context, the specific actions they took, the challenges they overcame, and the positive impact their actions had on the company and its customers.
hint: A four-sentence structure (role, situation, action, outcome) produces a storybook entry that is specific and memorable rather than a vague 'great job' anecdote.

title: Benefits enrolment reminder prompt
originalPrompt: You need to remind employees to enrol in the annual benefits window before it closes.
options: Write a benefits enrolment reminder email for employees. Cover: enrolment deadline (Friday 5pm), what happens if they miss it (defaulting to last year's choices), link to the benefits portal, top 3 benefits most employees change at this time (health cover level, pension contribution, dental add-on), and contact for help. Urgent but friendly tone. Max 150 words.
Write a reminder email about benefits enrolment.

Please write a comprehensive and informative email reminding employees that the annual benefits enrolment window is open and encouraging them to review all their benefits choices and make any changes they want before the deadline closes.
hint: A specific deadline, a consequence for inaction (default to last year), and three highlighted changes reduces the number of employees who miss the window or need to call HR.

title: Global talent mobility strategy prompt
originalPrompt: Your company operates in 8 countries and wants to build a global internal mobility programme.
options: Design a global internal mobility strategy for a company operating in the UK, US, Germany, Singapore, Australia, Canada, France, and India. Components: mobility pathways (developmental rotations vs permanent transfers vs project-based), policy framework (compensation approach, immigration support, relocation package tiers), tax and social security coordination (shadow payroll model), governance (HRBP network, mobility team), and employee experience (pre-move support, buddy network, repatriation plan). 2-year implementation roadmap.
Design a global mobility strategy for our international operations.
Create a comprehensive global talent mobility programme that allows employees to work across our international locations and covers all the policy, legal, tax, and logistical requirements.
hint: Naming all eight countries, five programme components with specific sub-elements (shadow payroll, buddy network, repatriation), and a 2-year roadmap means the strategy is scoped and executable rather than aspirational.
